title: Immediately disabling `fork` from a branch doesn't always work

features.name: fork with immediate disable from branch
features.description: Code should include a fork block with multiple branches where one branch immediately executes a disable fork statement (without any delay) targeting the parent fork block

features.name: disable fork block targeting parent fork
features.description: Code should include a disable statement that references a parent fork block label, where the disable executes at time 0 before other fork branches can register
features.snippet:
fork : fork_blk
  begin
    disable fork_blk;
  end
  begin
    #1;
  end
join_none

features.name: fork join_none with competing disable and delay branches
features.description: Code should include a fork join_none construct where one branch immediately disables the fork and another branch has a delay, creating a race condition in branch registration
